description: In an apartment living area, Monica sits at a dining table. Phoebe stands nearby, adjusting a bright orange tube attached to her shirt.

Phoebe unhooks the orange tube, rolling it up in her hands as she says, "All right, I got to go. I have to break up with Vince." Chandler, sitting at the table, turns his head toward her and asks, "Oh, so you're going with the, huh?"
Phoebe puts the rolled-up tube into her orange shoulder bag and says, "Yeah. I like Vince a lot, you know?"
"But Jason is so sensitive, you know?" Phoebe continues, crossing her arms. "And in the long run, I think sensitive is better than"
"having just, like, a really nice body," Phoebe adds, uncrossing her arms.
Phoebe stares straight ahead with her mouth slightly open. She swallows and then shifts her gaze back toward the group.
"Jason. Definitely Jason," Phoebe says, nodding. "Okay. Wish me luck," she adds, turning to leave. "Good luck," the others at the table reply.
Chandler takes a sip from a bottle. Ross and Joey stand nearby, holding glasses. Rachel sits at the table, smiling.
Rachel suddenly raises both arms into the air and shouts loudly. She then lowers her arms, her smile fading as she looks around.
Rachel briefly touches her mouth with one hand. "Sorry," she says, placing her hands on the table. "I was just imagining what it'd be like to catch the money bouquet."
The setting changes to the exterior of a red brick firehouse. An American flag hangs above the large red garage doors.
Inside the firehouse, a large red fire engine is parked. Phoebe walks toward a firefighter standing near the side of the engine.
"Is Vince here?" Phoebe asks the firefighter. He turns toward her and replies, "Oh, sure."
The firefighter looks over his shoulder and calls out loudly, "Vince."
"Yo," a voice replies from off-screen.
Vince slides down a brass pole from the ceiling, landing on the floor, and walks toward Phoebe.
"Wow. I didn't know you guys actually use those," Phoebe says, smiling as she watches him approach.
Vince continues walking toward Phoebe.
Vince walks up to Phoebe, gives her a quick kiss on the lips, and asks, "So, what's up?"
Phoebe looks down, rubs her hands together, and exhales audibly.
"Wow," Phoebe says, looking back down.
Phoebe keeps her gaze lowered, pressing her lips together.
"this isn't going to be easy," Phoebe says, shaking her head slightly.
Phoebe takes a short breath and looks up slightly.
Phoebe raises her hands slightly and says, "I don't think we should see each other anymore."
Vince keeps his gaze on her, his expression remaining still. He shifts his gaze slightly and nods slowly.
"Good deal," Vince says, nodding again with a slight shrug of his shoulders.
Vince lowers his head slightly. Phoebe looks at him, her eyebrows drawn together.
"I'm sorry," Phoebe says, leaning forward slightly.
"It's okay," Vince replies quickly, shaking his head.
Vince looks down, briefly shaking his head again.
Vince gestures with his hands while holding a pair of gloves. "It's just that I thought we had something pretty special here."
"And I felt like you were someone I could finally open up to. And" Vince continues, gesturing toward her.
"there's so much in me I haven't shared with you yet," Vince says, bringing his hand to his chest.
Phoebe's eyes widen slightly as she looks at Vince.
Phoebe places her hand on her chest and says, "Oh, my God," her voice strained. Vince raises his hands and says, "I'm sorry. I can't talk."
Vince turns his head away, raising one hand to his face.
"I'm gonna go write my" Vince says, holding up one hand as he begins to turn away.
Vince walks away, disappearing behind the fire engine. Phoebe stands still, her hand resting on her chest, watching him go.
Phoebe steps forward quickly, moving toward the stairs of the fire engine. The setting changes to the exterior of Central Perk coffee shop, visible through the front window.
Inside Central Perk, Phoebe, Ross, and Monica sit on an orange sofa. "telling you if you want to take care of that thing, you should go see my herbal guy," Phoebe says. "Thank you," Ross replies.
"But I want to remove it, Phoebe. So I don't want to make it savory," Ross says, gesturing with a small fork. Monica eats from a small plate on her lap.
Monica finishes chewing and swallows.
Monica points her fork toward Ross. "You know, when girls sleep with guys with weird things on their body they tell their friends about it," she says.
Ross narrows his eyes at Monica and reaches over.
Ross takes Monica's plate. "Give me this," he says, taking her fork as well.
Ross stands up and walks away from the sofa, carrying the plate and fork.
Rachel walks up to the sofa carrying a stack of magazines and says, "Hi."
"Okay, don't be mad at me, but I couldn't resist," Rachel says, sitting down in the spot Ross just vacated.
Rachel sets a magazine on Monica's lap. "Brides magazine?" Monica asks. "Yes, and I know that you said you would say no if he asked you but I'm sorry," Rachel replies.
"How great would you look walking down the aisle in this donna Karen" Rachel asks, pointing at the magazine page.
"oh, you so would," Phoebe chimes in, leaning over to look at the page.
Phoebe keeps her gaze on the magazine page.
"Oh, you should get that anyway" Phoebe adds, nodding toward the magazine.
Rachel and Monica look down at the magazine together. Monica then looks up at Phoebe.
"for clubbing," Phoebe finishes her sentence, smiling slightly.
Monica looks at Phoebe, her facial expression remaining still.
Monica closes the magazine and rests her hands on it. "It's so weird. I know what I said, but this morning, I was lying in bed."
"I was imagining what it would be like to" Monica continues.
"say yes," Monica adds. Rachel keeps her gaze on Monica.
Rachel turns her head slightly to look directly at Monica, while Phoebe watches Monica.
Monica gestures with her hands. "I know it's a little sudden and it's a little rushed and it's totally not like me to do something like this."
"But that doesn't mean I can't," Monica adds, looking toward Rachel.
"right? I mean, I'm crazy about Pete," Monica says, raising her eyebrows and looking between Rachel and Phoebe.
"And I know that we want the same things," Monica continues.
"And when I thought about saying yes, it made me really happy," Monica says, smiling softly.
Rachel gasps, covering her mouth with her hand. "Oh, my God," she says, her voice muffled.
Phoebe smiles and looks at Rachel, who keeps her hand over her mouth, her eyes wide.
"I know," Monica says, smiling and looking down.
Monica turns her head away, her smile fading slightly.
Monica stands up quickly. "I need more pie," she says, picking up a plate from the table.
Monica walks away from the sofa area. Rachel turns to look at Phoebe.
Phoebe leans forward slightly. "Hey, Mon, if you do get married, can I bring two guests?" she calls out.
Inside a coffee shop, Rachel and Phoebe sit on an orange sofa among other patrons. Rachel holds an open magazine on her lap.
Rachel looks at Phoebe and asks, "You didn't break up with that fireman?"
"No, that was my way of telling you," Phoebe replies.
Monica sits at an adjacent table, holding a plate.
Phoebe says, "Well, it turns out he's incredibly sensitive."
Gesturing with both hands, Phoebe says, "He keeps a journal and he paints. He even showed me charcoal drawings that he drew on me." Rachel says, "Wow."
Phoebe continues, "Yeah, well, he'd prefer watercolors"
Phoebe lowers her hands.
Phoebe finishes her sentence, "but he has easy access to a lot of charcoal." Rachel nods slightly.
Monica brings a forkful of food to her mouth.
Monica asks, "So then, are you going to dump Jason?"
"Well, yeah, because I have to break up with someone," Phoebe answers, gesturing. Rachel crosses her arms.
"Okay, so Jason is sensitive. But now so is Vince," Phoebe says, raising one middle finger, then the other.
Pointing at her fingers, Phoebe adds, "Plus, Vince has the body, you know, so"
Phoebe points her index fingers toward each other.
"really, just about the math," Phoebe says.
Rachel nods.
The setting changes to the exterior of a multi-story brick building with fire escapes, then changes to an interior hallway where Phoebe knocks on a gray door.
Phoebe calls out, "Jason?" Jason replies from inside, "Yeah, come on in." Phoebe pushes the door open.
Phoebe steps inside. Jason, shirtless and wearing a tool belt, turns from a wooden shelving unit holding a power drill.
Jason walks toward Phoebe, holding the drill. Phoebe's mouth falls open slightly.
"Phoebe, you sounded kind of serious on the phone. Is anything wrong?" Jason asks. Phoebe shakes her head and replies, "Nah."
Phoebe smiles at Jason. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to an office with shelves of apothecary jars and a padded examination table. A skull sits on a counter as Ross walks into the room.
Ross pauses, looking around. He walks toward a wooden cabinet containing many small drawers.
Ross pulls open a small drawer, removing a small wooden box. Guru Saj enters through the door behind him.
"You must be Ross," Guru Saj says, extending his arms.
Ross turns toward him, holding the wooden box.
Ross says, "Hi." Guru Saj presses his hands together and says, "I am Guru Saj."
Ross closes the box and sets it down on the cabinet.
"I gotta tell you," Ross says.
"I've never been to a guru before," Ross adds.
Guru Saj says, "Well, relax. If it makes you feel any better I've attended some of the finest medical schools in Central America."
Guru Saj gestures toward the examination table and adds, "Well, then,"
"let's take a look at the skin abnormality of yours," Guru Saj finishes.
Ross begins to loosen his belt.
"Come on, have a seat," Guru Saj says, patting the table.
Ross sits on the edge of the examination table. Guru Saj pulls Ross's shirt up and examines his lower back.
"As I suspected, it's a kundas," Guru Saj says.
Ross turns his head to look over his shoulder.
Ross asks, "What's a kundas?" Guru Saj stands up straight and replies, "I don't know. What's a kundas with you?" Audience laughter plays.
Guru Saj smiles. Ross stares at him without smiling. Audience laughter continues.
"Please lie down. I've got a salve that ought to shrink that right up," Guru Saj says, walking over to the shelves.
Ross lies face down on the table and says, "Well, I guess it's worth a try."

Guru Saj applies a substance from a jar onto Ross's back, saying, "Oh, sure. We should see results." Ross suddenly yells, "Whoa," and thrashes. Guru Saj jumps back, adding, "Clearly not the way to go."
Ross lifts his upper body with his arms, looking back over his shoulder.
"What?" Ross asks, breathing heavily.
Guru Saj looks at Ross.
"We appear to have angered it," Guru Saj says.
Ross stares at Guru Saj.
"We angered it," Ross repeats, his pitch rising.
"Oh, I think I see the problem," Guru Saj says.
Guru Saj straightens his posture.
"And I'm afraid we're going to have to use a much stronger tool," Guru Saj says.
Guru Saj looks at Ross, pausing.
"love," Guru Saj says. Ross looks away, exhaling audibly.
Ross lowers his head and says, "Oh, God." Guru Saj steps closer, saying, "Ross, often these things are nothing more than just negative energy trying to escape your body."
"Now, this is not going to come off," Guru Saj continues.
"unless you learn to love it, my friend," Guru Saj finishes, leaning forward.
"So let me hear you say you love the kundas," Guru Saj says.
Ross looks at Guru Saj with his mouth open.
In a room with wooden apothecary-style drawers, Ross lies on his stomach on an examination table. Guru Saj stands over him. Ross says, "I love the kundas."
Guru Saj hovers his hands over Ross's back.

Guru Saj presses down firmly on Ross's back and says, "Ross, there is absolutely no way this is going to come off unless you start to ow. Oops." Ross exclaims "Ow," lifting his head, as Guru Saj says "Oops."
Ross turns his head to look over his shoulder and asks, "What was that?"
Guru Saj begins to stand upright.
Guru Saj stands fully upright and says, "Well, it's gone," as audience laughter is heard.
Ross raises himself slightly on his elbows.
Ross asks, "How's that?" Guru Saj gestures to his wrist and replies, "Got caught in my watch."
Ross smiles.
Ross begins to sit up and says, "Hey."

The scene changes to a nighttime exterior view of a city skyline. The setting then changes to an indoor room featuring bookshelves and a globe. Pete, wearing a tuxedo, and Monica, wearing a dress and a shawl, walk through a doorway.
Pete says, "Lights," and the illumination in the room visibly dims.
Monica looks around the dimly lit room with a slight smile.
Pete says, "Romantic lights."
Monica touches her neck while smiling.
Monica says, "Nice."
Pete places a hand on Monica's waist.
Pete guides her forward as he says, "So there was this thing I wanted to talk to you about."
Monica turns toward Pete and responds, "Oh, right. I completely forgot about that."
Pete gestures toward a black leather sofa.
Pete and Monica sit down on the sofa. Pete says, "Well, I've been doing a lot of thinking."
Pete looks at Monica.
Pete says, "And I look at my life," to which Monica replies, "Yeah?"
Pete nods slightly.
Pete looks at Monica and says, "And I feel like I've conquered the business world."
Pete shifts his posture.
Pete gestures slightly with one hand and continues, "And I feel like I've conquered the intellectual world."
Pete reaches out toward Monica.
Pete takes Monica's hands in his and says, "And now I have the most beautiful woman in the world."
Monica smiles silently at Pete.
Monica replies, "Wow."
Pete raises his index finger.
Pete says, "But there's one thing missing."
Monica leans her head slightly.
Monica asks, "What's that?"
Pete closes his eyes briefly.
Pete leans in closer and says, "It's time for me to conquer the physical world."
Monica stares at Pete in silence.
Monica says, "Okay."
Monica looks at Pete as he maintains his hold on her hands.
Pete says, "Monica, I want to become."
Pete continues to look intently at Monica.
Pete finishes his sentence, saying, "The Ultimate Fighting Champion."
Monica's eyes widen slightly.
Monica leans back and asks, "You want a what?"
Pete releases her hands.
Pete says, "I want to become the Ultimate Fighting Champion. It's the most intense physical competition in the world," as he quickly stands up from the sofa.
Pete spreads his arms outward and adds, "It's banned in 49 states."
Monica looks up at him and asks, "What are you talking about?"
Pete paces slightly and gestures with his hands, saying, "Okay, my trainer, Hochi is teaching me a combination of ji Kundo and Brazilian street fighting."
Pete places his hands on his hips and continues, "I even had my own octagon training ring designed."
Monica asks, "And I suppose you used a ring designer for that?" Pete immediately replies, "Yeah."
Pete lowers himself back toward the sofa.
Pete kneels in front of Monica, takes her hands again, and says, "Monica, I want you there in the front row when I win."
Pete moves his hand toward her neck.
Pete rests his hand on the back of Monica's neck and says, "I want you close enough to smell the blood."
Pete maintains steady eye contact.
Pete asks, "What do you think?"
Monica presses her lips together briefly.
Monica says, "My parents will be so happy."
Pete kisses Monica's hand. The scene changes to a daytime aerial view of dense city buildings.
The setting changes to the interior of Central Perk coffee shop, where Phoebe sits on a stool playing an acoustic guitar in front of an audience.
Phoebe sings, "Underwear creeping at my butt," while strumming the guitar.
Jason, wearing a black t-shirt and white trousers, walks into the coffee shop.
Phoebe continues singing, "Crazy underwear, always in a rut, crazy underwear," as Jason smiles and looks toward her.
Jason approaches the seating area.
Phoebe's facial expression tightens, and she sings her inner thoughts to the melody: "Oh, no. What is he doing here?"
Phoebe continues singing her thoughts: "Just keep playing. Just keep playing. You'll get through this. You'll be fine."
Phoebe strums her guitar silently.
Phoebe sings a single sustained note for "Heaven."
Phoebe continues strumming the guitar silently as Jason watches her.
Phoebe finishes her song by singing, "Is mando."
Audience applause begins as Phoebe stops playing the guitar.
Phoebe speaks into the microphone, saying, "Okay, thank you."
Phoebe begins to stand up.
Phoebe adds, "And as always, no one talked to me after the show," before unstrapping her guitar and stepping down from the stool.
Jason claps his hands and walks over to Phoebe.
Jason says, "Hey."
Jason gestures slightly and says, "Passing by and saw you were playing tonight. It's kind of cool seeing you up there."
Vince, wearing a blue button-down shirt, approaches them.
Vince says, "Whoa."
Vince asks, "Hey, what's going on here?" Jason looks at Phoebe and asks, "Who's this guy?"
Phoebe responds, "I don't know. He just started kissing me."
Phoebe points at Jason and says to Vince, "Get him. Get him, Vince." Audience laughter sounds.
In a crowded coffee shop, Jason and Vince stand facing Phoebe. Jason asks, "What?"
Phoebe exhales audibly, lowering her head slightly.
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she speaks to them. "Yeah. Okay, I've been dating both of you and it's been really horrible because it's been"
Smiling, Phoebe lowers her gaze and adds, "a lot of fun for me,"
Phoebe brings her hands to her face, laughing. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe stops laughing, looks at them, and says, "but"
Phoebe raises her hands slightly.
Gesturing toward both men, Phoebe says, "I like you both, and I didn't know how to"
Phoebe drops her hands as audience laughter plays.
Phoebe shrugs and says, "I'm sorry."
Phoebe brings her hands to her chest, saying, "I'm just I'm terrible. I'm a terrible person. I'm terrible."
Vince reaches out with both hands, palms facing down, saying, "Phoebe. Phoebe. Relax."
Vince lowers his hands.
Vince says, "It's okay. I mean, we never said this was exclusive."
Jason gestures toward Vince and Phoebe, adding, "Yeah, neither did we."
Jason gestures to Phoebe and says, "Give yourself a"
Phoebe looks at Jason. Audience laughter plays.
Jason gestures between himself and Phoebe. "I mean, you know, we haven't been going out that long. Come on, we haven't even slept together yet."
Vince looks at Jason.
Vince turns his head toward Jason and asks, "You haven't?"
Jason looks at Vince in silence, then back to Phoebe. Audience laughter is heard.
Jason asks Vince, "You have?" He then turns slightly away, raising his hands, and adds, "Okay, well, this is none of my business."
Jason turns and walks away through the coffee shop. Phoebe watches him leave. Audience laughter plays.
Jason returns, walking quickly toward Vince. "I can't believe this. You slept with him? Well, I made you a candlelight dinner in the park."
Jason looks at Phoebe.
Jason points at Phoebe and says, "You know, Phoebe, I'm going to make this real easy for you."
Jason turns and walks away again, fully exiting the scene. Vince and Phoebe watch him leave. Audience laughter continues.
Phoebe turns to Vince, shrugging, and says, "Well, that could have been really awkward." Audience laughter plays.
Vince puts his hand to his mouth, looks down briefly, then looks back at Phoebe.
Vince asks her, "You made him a candlelight dinner in the park?"
Phoebe nods slightly.
Speaking faster than before, Phoebe gestures with her hands. "Yeah, but I can do that for you. I'm going to do that for you."
Vince raises a hand and steps back. "Yeah. I can't believe I ever went out with somebody who would actually have an open flame in the middle of a wooded area."
Vince turns and walks away. Phoebe exhales audibly, looking down. Audience laughter plays.
The setting changes to a clinic room lined with medicine cabinets. Chandler and Joey sit on an examination table next to a white duck. Joey strokes the duck's back.
Continuing to stroke the duck, Joey says, "Everything's going to be all right."
Joey stops stroking the duck.
Chandler looks at the duck and says, "Okay, duck."
Guru Saj, wearing glasses and a long white tunic, enters the room. Joey and Chandler look toward him.
Guru Saj stands by the door and says, "Hello."
Stepping forward, Guru Saj says, "I am Guru Saj."
Chandler leans back slightly and says, "Whoa."
Chandler and Joey look at Guru Saj.
Chandler points to the duck and says to Guru Saj, "He's supposed to be a duck, right? Because otherwise this is way out of my league." Audience laughter plays.
Joey gestures to the duck, then to himself. "Yeah, he's got a really bad cough. And our vet, he can't do anything about it. Is there something you can do?"
Guru Saj looks at the duck.
Guru Saj steps closer and says, "Let me see."
Guru Saj leans toward the duck.
Guru Saj says again, "Let me see."
Guru Saj stands near the duck, clasping his hands together as he looks at it.
Guru Saj asks Joey, "Do you think you could get him to eat a bathroom?" A duck quacks audibly. Joey stares at him.
Joey and Chandler remain silent. The screen fades to black at 02:15.
The screen remains black.
The screen remains black.
